title: Doflamingo Is Wounded
description: Pages 9–13 focus on doflamingo is wounded, keeping the Dressrosa conflict and character roles clear for display.
scene: This part follows Doflamingo, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6 as law uses his hidden trump card against Doflamingo, but the tyrant's power keeps the final battle from ending.

dialogue.speaker: Doflamingo
dialogue.text: Why do you bear a heart on your back, law!?

dialogue.speaker: King Riku
dialogue.text: King Riku! My apologies for keeping you waiting! I shall ac- company you!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: We've still got people to save!

dialogue.speaker: Doflamingo Family
dialogue.text: It's true that the family's still-standing powerhouses...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Our forces have taken a hit as well.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: took you long enough, straw hat.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I released Bellamy a while back...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Did he manage to find freedom?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Quit playing dumb! I know exactly what you're up to!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You knew Bellamy could never run!

dialogue.speaker: Doflamingo
dialogue.text: Fufufu... so he chose not to run, did he?

summary: Doflamingo Is Wounded advances the Dressrosa story through Doflamingo, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4, with unclear voices kept readable rather than forced into unsafe speaker assignments.
